response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong software craftsmanship, TDD discipline, and notable geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk), which aligns well with the routing/navigation data context. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and the resume shows only brief, decade-old C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis, with no Rust and no recent systems-level work. Recent experience skews heavily to TypeScript/Node and DLang rather than the low-latency C++ pipelines this position demands.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput service experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; C/C++ work is from 2014-2015
No Rust experience listed as an acceptable alternative
No demonstrated work on production navigation/routing tile pipelines

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ or Rust
